questions.question:
In a recently conducted national entrance test, boys constituted 60% of those who appeared for the test. Girls constituted the remaining candidates and they accounted for 60% of the qualified candidates.

Which one of the following is the correct logical inference based on the information provided in the above passage?

A. Equal number of boys and girls qualified
B. Equal number of boys and girls appeared for the test
C. The number of boys qualified is less than the number of girls qualified
D. The number of boys who qualified is less than the number of girls who qualified
questions.answer: D. The number of boys who qualified is less than the number of girls who qualified

understand.plain: The passage states that boys made up 60% of all test-takers, so girls were 40% of test-takers. Yet girls accounted for 60% of all qualified candidates, meaning boys made up only 40% of qualifiers. Since boys outnumbered girls among test-takers but are underrepresented among qualifiers, the absolute number of boys who qualified must be less than the absolute number of girls who qualified. This is a straightforward logical inference about proportions versus absolute counts.

keywords.explain: A conclusion that must be true given the stated facts, not merely a guess or assumption.
keywords.example: If 60% of qualifiers are girls, then fewer than half of qualifiers are boys, so boys qualified fewer in number than girls.

keywords.explain: A percentage describes a share of a total; comparing percentages across different totals can yield counterintuitive results about absolute numbers.
keywords.example: Girls are 40% of test-takers but 60% of qualifiers, so the qualifying rate for girls is far higher than for boys.

keywords.explain: The fraction of a group that meets the qualifying threshold; a higher rate means a group qualifies in greater proportion regardless of its original size.
keywords.example: Even though fewer girls appeared, a higher fraction of them qualified, so they dominate among qualifiers.

understand.visual_alt: No figure needed; the question is purely text-based passage reading.

given.aim: Identify the correct logical inference from the given passage about boys and girls in a national entrance test.

terms.meaning: 60% of all candidates who appeared were boys, so girls were 40%.
terms.connects: Sets the ratio of boys to girls in the appearing pool.

terms.meaning: 60% of all qualified candidates were girls, so boys were only 40% of qualifiers.
terms.connects: Directly tells us girls outnumber boys in the qualified group.

terms.meaning: The actual number (not percentage) of boys and girls who qualified.
terms.example: Boys = 40% of qualifiers, girls = 60% of qualifiers, so boys < girls in absolute count.
terms.connects: Determines which option is logically correct.

given.plan: Assign a hypothetical total (e.g., 1000 appeared, 100 qualified) and compute the absolute numbers of boys and girls who qualified using the given percentages, then match to the answer choices.

steps.title: Set up hypothetical totals
steps.note: These numbers are hypothetical; the percentages alone determine the inference.

steps.title: Identify composition of qualifiers
steps.apply: Girls accounted for 60% of qualifiers. Therefore boys accounted for 40% of qualifiers. Regardless of the total number of qualifiers Q, boys who qualified = 0.4Q and girls who qualified = 0.6Q.
steps.note: Since 0.4Q < 0.6Q for any positive Q, boys who qualified < girls who qualified absolutely.

steps.title: Evaluate each option
steps.apply: A. Equal boys/girls qualified — false (40% != 60%). B. Equal appeared — false (60% boys). C/D. Both say boys qualified less than girls — true in both wording; option D is the precisely worded correct choice.
steps.note: Options C and D say essentially the same thing; D is the exact statement matching the answer key.

solution.result: The number of boys who qualified is less than the number of girls who qualified. Answer: D.

questions.formula_note: This question requires logical reasoning about percentages; no algebraic formula is needed beyond direct comparison of proportions.

questions.question:
The country of Zombieland is in distress since more than 75% of its working population is suffering from serious health issues. Studies conducted by competent health experts concluded that a complete lack of physical exercise among its working population was one of the leading causes of their health issues. As one of the measures to address the problem, the Government of Zombieland has decided to provide monetary incentives to those who ride bicycles to work.

Based only on the information provided above, which one of the following statements can be logically inferred with certainty?

A. All the working population of Zombieland will henceforth ride bicycles to work.
B. Riding bicycles will ensure that all the working population of Zombieland is free of health issues.
C. The health experts suggested to the Government of Zombieland to declare riding bicycles as mandatory.
D. The Government of Zombieland believes that riding bicycles is a form of physical exercise.
questions.answer: D. The Government of Zombieland believes that riding bicycles is a form of physical exercise.

understand.plain: The passage describes a health crisis in Zombieland caused largely by lack of physical exercise, and states the government decided to incentivize cycling to work as a remedy. The only statement that can be inferred with certainty is that the government sees cycling as a form of physical exercise, since that is the unstated premise behind choosing cycling as the intervention. The other options make claims not supported by the passage.

keywords.explain: A statement is a certain inference only if it must be true given the passage; it cannot merely be plausible or possible.
keywords.example: If the government offers incentives for cycling to counter lack of exercise, they must believe cycling IS exercise.

keywords.explain: A belief or fact that is not stated explicitly but must be true for the argument to make sense.
keywords.example: The government's decision to promote cycling implicitly assumes cycling counts as physical exercise.

keywords.explain: Drawing a conclusion that goes beyond what the evidence supports, especially using words like 'all' or 'ensure'.
keywords.example: Saying cycling will ensure ALL workers are free of health issues overgeneralises beyond the passage's claim.

understand.visual_alt: No figure needed; this is a passage-based verbal reasoning question.

given.aim: Determine which single statement is a certain logical inference from the passage about Zombieland's health crisis and government response.

terms.meaning: More than 75% of working population has serious health issues; lack of physical exercise is a leading cause.
terms.example: Three out of four workers are affected, and inactivity is a key driver.
terms.connects: Motivates the government's action toward promoting physical activity.

terms.meaning: Monetary incentives for those who ride bicycles to work.
terms.example: Workers who cycle receive financial rewards as encouragement.
terms.connects: The government treats cycling as a suitable physical exercise remedy.

terms.meaning: An inference follows from facts; an assumption is a hidden premise required for the argument.
terms.example: Incentivising cycling as a fix for lack-of-exercise implies the government believes cycling = exercise.
terms.connects: This is the key reasoning step to arrive at option D.

given.plan: Evaluate each option against the passage. Reject any that go beyond what is stated (e.g., uses 'all', 'ensure', 'mandatory'). Identify the option whose truth is required by the government's decision itself.

steps.title: Eliminate options using 'all' or 'ensure'
steps.formula_raw: F logically implies E iff E is true in every assignment where F is true
steps.apply: Option A says ALL workers will ride bicycles — not stated; incentives do not guarantee 100% compliance. Option B says cycling will ENSURE freedom from all health issues — too strong; exercise is just one cause. Both A and B are not certain inferences.
steps.note: Words like 'all', 'ensure', 'will' make a statement too strong to be a certain logical inference from limited data.

steps.title: Check option C
steps.apply: Option C says health experts suggested declaring cycling mandatory. The passage only says experts identified lack of exercise as a cause; it does not say they recommended cycling specifically or making it mandatory. Option C is not supported.
steps.note: The suggestion to incentivise cycling came from the government, not necessarily the health experts.

steps.title: Confirm option D as the certain inference
steps.formula_raw: F logically implies E iff E is true in every assignment where F is true
steps.apply: The government chose cycling (as a remedy for lack of physical exercise) => the government must believe cycling is a form of physical exercise. This is necessarily true for their action to be rational. Option D follows with certainty.
steps.note: This is an inference about the government's implicit belief, which is required by the logic of their decision.

solution.result: Option D: The Government of Zombieland believes that riding bicycles is a form of physical exercise. Answer: D.

questions.formula_note: Logical entailment is used to test whether each option must be true given the passage, eliminating overly broad claims and confirming the implicit-belief inference.

questions.question:
Mosquitoes pose a threat to human health. Controlling mosquitoes using chemicals may have undesired consequences. In Florida, authorities have used genetically modified mosquitoes to control the overall mosquito population. It remains to be seen if this novel approach has unforeseen consequences.

Which one of the following is the correct inference based on the above passage?

A. Using chemicals to kill mosquitoes is better than using genetically modified mosquitoes because genetic engineering is dangerous
B. Using genetically modified mosquitoes is better than using chemicals to kill mosquitoes because they do not have any side effects
C. Both using genetically modified mosquitoes and chemicals have undesired consequences and can be dangerous
D. Using chemicals to kill mosquitoes may have undesired consequences but it is not clear if using genetically modified mosquitoes has any negative consequence
questions.answer: D. Using chemicals to kill mosquitoes may have undesired consequences but it is not clear if using genetically modified mosquitoes has any negative consequence

understand.plain: The passage says chemical methods may have undesired consequences. The GM mosquito approach is called 'novel' and it 'remains to be seen' whether it has unforeseen consequences — meaning uncertainty, not confirmed harm or confirmed safety. The correct inference must respect this uncertainty without overstating either side. Option D is the only one that accurately captures both facts without going beyond the text.

keywords.explain: A statement that accurately reflects what is stated in the passage — no more, no less.
keywords.example: Saying GM mosquitoes 'may' have consequences matches the passage; saying they 'do not' have side effects does not.

keywords.explain: Phrases like 'it remains to be seen' or 'may have' signal that outcomes are unknown, not confirmed.
keywords.example: 'It remains to be seen if this novel approach has unforeseen consequences' means we do not yet know if it is harmful.

keywords.explain: Drawing a conclusion stronger than what the passage allows, e.g., claiming something is definitively better or worse.
keywords.example: Saying GM mosquitoes are better because they have NO side effects goes beyond the passage.

understand.visual_alt: No figure needed; this is a passage-based logical inference question.

given.aim: Find the correct inference about mosquito control methods from the passage.

terms.meaning: Using pesticides or chemicals to kill mosquitoes; stated to MAY have undesired consequences.
terms.example: Chemical spraying can harm non-target species or contaminate water.
terms.connects: Establishes a known potential downside for chemical methods.

terms.meaning: Mosquitoes engineered to reduce the population; a novel approach used in Florida.
terms.example: GM males may be released to reduce breeding, but long-term ecosystem effects are unknown.
terms.connects: The outcome of GM mosquitoes is uncertain, not confirmed harmful or harmless.

terms.meaning: The GM approach is new and its consequences (if any) are not yet determined.
terms.example: 'Remains to be seen' = future uncertainty, not current confirmed harm.
terms.connects: Rules out options claiming GM methods have no side effects OR are definitely harmful.

given.plan: Map each answer option to the exact claims in the passage and eliminate those that add information not in the text or contradict it.

steps.title: Assess option A
steps.apply: Option A claims chemicals are BETTER and that genetic engineering is DANGEROUS. The passage does not compare the two methods or state genetic engineering is dangerous. Eliminate A.
steps.note: The passage is neutral about which approach is better.

steps.title: Assess option B
steps.apply: Option B claims GM mosquitoes are better and have NO side effects. The passage explicitly states it 'remains to be seen' — meaning side effects are unknown, not absent. Eliminate B.
steps.note: Claiming no side effects contradicts the passage's expressed uncertainty.

steps.title: Assess option C
steps.apply: Option C claims BOTH methods have undesired consequences and can be dangerous. For chemicals, the passage says 'may have' — possible but not certain. For GM mosquitoes, no consequences have been confirmed yet. Eliminate C.
steps.note: The passage does not confirm harm from either method with certainty.

steps.title: Confirm option D
steps.formula_raw: An implication X -> Y is false only when X is true and Y is false; in every other case it is true.
steps.apply: Option D states: chemicals MAY have undesired consequences (matches 'may have') AND it is NOT CLEAR if GM mosquitoes have negative consequences (matches 'remains to be seen'). Both halves align precisely with the passage. D is the correct inference.
steps.note: Option D is the only choice that does not introduce information beyond the passage.

solution.result: Using chemicals to kill mosquitoes may have undesired consequences but it is not clear if using genetically modified mosquitoes has any negative consequence. Answer: D.

questions.formula_note: The truth-condition of implication helps verify which claims the passage actually supports versus which are false or unsupported.

questions.question:
Non-performing Assets (NPA) of a bank in India is defined as an asset, which remains unpaid by a borrower for a certain period of time in terms of interest, principal, or both. Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has changed the definition of NPA thrice during 1993-2004, in terms of the holding period of loans. The holding period was reduced by one quarter each time. In 1993 the holding period of loans was four quarters (360 days).

Based on the above paragraph, the holding period of loans in 2004 after the third revision was _______ days.

A. 45
B. 90
C. 135
D. 180

understand.plain: The holding period for NPA classification started at four quarters (360 days) in 1993. The RBI reduced it by one quarter each time, and the definition was changed three times. Starting from 4 quarters, after three reductions of 1 quarter each, the holding period becomes 4 - 3 = 1 quarter. One quarter equals 360/4 = 90 days. This is a straightforward arithmetic sequence problem embedded in a factual passage.

keywords.explain: A loan or advance where the borrower has not paid interest or principal for a defined holding period.
keywords.example: If a borrower does not pay for 90 days (1 quarter), the asset is classified as non-performing after the third revision.

keywords.meaning: The number of days/quarters a loan must remain unpaid before being classified as NPA.
keywords.example: In 1993, a loan had to be unpaid for 360 days (4 quarters) to be called NPA.
keywords.connects: This is the value being reduced with each revision.

keywords.term: Reduction by one quarter each time
keywords.explain: Each of the three RBI revisions removed exactly one quarter (90 days) from the holding period.
keywords.connects: Determines the final holding period after all three revisions.

understand.visual_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 520 140' width='520' height='140'><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect width='520' height='140' rx='10' fill='var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8)'/><rect x='10' y='20' width='110' height='90' rx='8' class='bg'/><text x='65' y='48' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>1993</text><text x='65' y='72' text-anchor='middle' class='big ink'>4Q</text><text x='65' y='96' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>360 days</text><text x='135' y='72' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl mut'>-1Q</text><rect x='155' y='20' width='110' height='90' rx='8' class='bg'/><text x='210' y='48' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Rev 1</text><text x='210' y='72' text-anchor='middle' class='big ink'>3Q</text><text x='210' y='96' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>270 days</text><text x='280' y='72' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl mut'>-1Q</text><rect x='300' y='20' width='110' height='90' rx='8' class='bg'/><text x='355' y='48' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Rev 2</text><text x='355' y='72' text-anchor='middle' class='big ink'>2Q</text><text x='355' y='96' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>180 days</text><text x='425' y='72' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl mut'>-1Q</text><rect x='445' y='20' width='65' height='90' rx='8' class='hi'/><text x='477' y='48' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>Rev 3</text><text x='477' y='72' text-anchor='middle' class='big ink'>1Q</text><text x='477' y='96' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>90 days</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Timeline showing the holding period reducing from 4 quarters (360 days) in 1993 down to 1 quarter (90 days) after the third revision, with each arrow marked minus 1 quarter.

given.aim: Calculate the holding period of NPA loans in 2004 after the third revision by the Reserve Bank of India.

terms.meaning: Four quarters = 360 days; a loan unpaid for 360 days was classified as NPA.
terms.connects: This is the starting value for the arithmetic sequence.

terms.meaning: Each RBI revision reduces the holding period by exactly one quarter (90 days).
terms.connects: Applied three times to reach the final value.

terms.meaning: The RBI revised the NPA definition exactly three times between 1993 and 2004.
terms.example: Three reductions of 1 quarter each = total reduction of 3 quarters.
terms.connects: Determines how many times the holding period was reduced.

given.plan: Start with 4 quarters (360 days). Subtract 1 quarter for each of the 3 revisions to find the final holding period, then convert to days using 1 quarter = 90 days.

steps.title: Identify initial holding period in quarters
steps.apply: In 1993, holding period = 4 quarters. One quarter = 360/4 = 90 days.
steps.note: The problem states 4 quarters = 360 days, confirming 1 quarter = 90 days.

steps.title: Apply three reductions of one quarter each
steps.formula_raw: Final quarters = Initial quarters - Number of revisions = 4 - 3 = 1
steps.apply: After Revision 1: 4 - 1 = 3 quarters. After Revision 2: 3 - 1 = 2 quarters. After Revision 3 (2004): 2 - 1 = 1 quarter.
steps.note: Each of the three revisions reduces the holding period by exactly 1 quarter.

steps.title: Convert final quarters to days
steps.note: The answer 90 days matches option B.

solution.result: The holding period of loans in 2004 after the third revision was 90 days. Answer: B.

questions.formula_note: No standard formula ID applies; the solution uses simple arithmetic subtraction on a sequence of quarter reductions.
